name: [68Ga]Ga-D0103
radiochemical_method_description: 68Ga-radiolabeling was done by mixing a solution of sodium acetate (30 µL, 155 mg/mL in water) and 300 µL of generator eluate (80–85 MBq) with either 10 µg (for in vivo imaging) or 25 µg (for ex vivo biodistribution studies) of D0103 or Trivehexin. The mixture was incubated at 95°C for 15 min. Sodium acetate (100 µL) was then added to raise the pH to ~ 6.

name: [68Ga]Ga-Trivehexin
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling for preclinical experiments and determination of n-octanol–PBS distribution coefficients (log D7.4) was done as described . Applying this protocol, 5 nmol (0.5 nmol for cellular assays in order to maximize molar activity) of TRAP(AvB6)3 or Trivehexin were labeled with 68 Ga at pH 2, resulting RCY > 95%.

name: [90Y]Y-3PRGD2
radiochemical_method_description: For 111In and 90Y radiolabeling, 74-370 MBq of111InCl3 or 90YCl3 was diluted in 300μL of 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer (pH 5.2) and added to the DOTA-peptide conjugates (5 μg ofpeptide per mCi of111InCl3 or 90YCl3).

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-5G
radiochemical_method_description: Gallium-68 was eluted from a 68Ge/68Ga generator ((Eckert and Ziegler, Berlin, Germany) using 5 mL of 0.1 N HCl, and 1.5 mL fraction containing the bulk radioactivity was used. Radiolabeling was conducted at pH 4.0 in 1M HEPES buffer.

name: [68Ga]GaDOTA-ABM-5G
radiochemical_method_description: Gallium-68 was eluted from a 68Ge/68Ga generator ((Eckert and Ziegler, Berlin, Germany) using 5 mL of 0.1 N HCl, and 1.5 mL fraction containing the bulk radioactivity was used. Radiolabeling was conducted at pH 4.0 in 1M HEPES buffer.

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-5G
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling of peptides 1 and 2 with lutetium-177 (ITM (Garching, Germany) was conducted at pH 5.0 in 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer by adding 1 (44 μg) or 2 (46 μg) to [177Lu]LuCl3 (a 148 MBq aliquot of the commercial [177Lu]LuCl3 in 0.04 N HCl in 100-150 μL buffer) and ascorbic acid (25 μL of a 5 mg/mL aq. solution).

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-ABM-5G
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling of peptides 1 and 2 with lutetium-177 (ITM (Garching, Germany) was conducted at pH 5.0 in 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer by adding 1 (44 μg) or 2 (46 μg) to [177Lu]LuCl3 (a 148 MBq aliquot of the commercial [177Lu]LuCl3 in 0.04 N HCl in 100-150 μL buffer) and ascorbic acid (25 μL of a 5 mg/mL aq. solution).

name: [68Ga]NODAGA-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: to 10 μl NODAGA-RGD (1 μg/μl in water) 60 μl 1.9 M sodium acetate solution and 300 μl 68GaCl3 of the main fraction of the generator eluate (approx. 100–200 MBq in 0.1 M HCl) were added. The solution was allowed to react for 15 min at room temperature.

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: to 10 μl NODAGA-RGD (1 μg/μl in water) 60 μl 1.9 M sodium acetate solution and 300 μl 68GaCl3 of the main fraction of the generator eluate (approx. 100–200 MBq in 0.1 M HCl) were added. The solution was allowed to react for 15 min at room temperature.

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-E{E[c(RGDyK)]2}2
radiochemical_method_description:
In brief, 20 mLof 64CuCl2 (74 MBq in 0.1N HCl) were diluted in 400 mL of 0.1 mol/L sodium acetate buffer (pH 6.5) and added to the DOTA-RGD multimer (a 1 mg/mL
solution of peptide was made and separated into aliquots; 5 mgof DOTA-RGD tetramer and 10 mg of DOTA-RGD octamer per 37 MBq of 64Cu were used for the labeling). The reaction mixture was incubated for 1 h at 50 C.

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-E(E{E[c(RGDyK)]2}2)2
radiochemical_method_description:
In brief, 20 mLof 64CuCl2 (74 MBq in 0.1N HCl) were diluted in 400 mL of 0.1 mol/L sodium acetate buffer (pH 6.5) and added to the DOTA-RGD multimer (a 1 mg/mL
solution of peptide was made and separated into aliquots; 5 mgof DOTA-RGD tetramer and 10 mg of DOTA-RGD octamer per 37 MBq of 64Cu were used for the labeling). The reaction mixture was incubated for 1 h at 50 C.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NGR-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: In brief, 2 μL of NGR-RGD, NOTA-c(RGDyK) or NOTA-c(KNGRE) (2 mM) was added to 150 μL buffer (0.25 M sodium acetate, pH 6.8). Subsequently, 500 μL [68Ga]GaCl3 in 0.05 M HCl (180–400 MBq) was added and mixed. The final pH of the reaction mixture was approximately 4.0. Then, the reaction mixture was heated to 90°C and reacted for 5 min. After cooling to room temperature, 68Ga-NGR-RGD, 68Ga-NOTA-c(RGDyK) (denoted as 68Ga-RGD), and 68Ga-NOTA-c(KNGRE) (denoted as 68Ga-NGR) were obtained and used for the subsequent studies without

name: [68Ga]Ga-sCD146
radiochemical_method_description: gallium-68 chloride (68GaCl3, 200.7 ± 41 MBq/500 µL) was buffered with a fresh 2M ammonium acetate solution (pH 7.4) and 10 µg of RGD2 precursor (catalogue number 9804, purchased at ABX, Radeberg, Germany) was added. The reaction mixture was stirred at room temperature for 15 min.

name: [68Ga]Ga-(RGD)2
radiochemical_method_description: Gallium chloride  was obtained from the elution of a commercial TiO2-based68Ge/68Ga generator (Galliapharm, Eckert-Ziegler) using 0.1 N HCl, and buffered with a fresh 4 M ammonium acetate solution (pH 7.4) and added to sCD146 precursor (15 渭g), reaching a final pH of 6.0. The reaction mixture was stirred at room temperature for 15 min with gentle agitation every 5 min.

name: [99mTc]Tc(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS)
radiochemical_method_description: To a vial filled with a 1.0 mL solution (pH ) 4.8) containing 5 mg of TPPTS, 6.5 mg of tricine, 40 mg of mannitol, 38.5 mg of disodium succinate hexahydrate, and 12.7 mg of succinic acid were added 0.2 mL of HYNIC-tetramer solution (100 µg/ mL in water) and 0.3 mL of Na[99mTcO4] solution (370-1850 MBq/mL). The vial was heated at 100 °C for 20-25 min in a lead-shielded water bath. After heating, the vial was placed back into the lead pig and allowed to stand at room temperature for ∼10 min.

name: [67Ga]Ga-CD6
radiochemical_method_description: The purified NOTA-peptides (20-40 µg) were incubated with 67GaCl3 or 67Ga-citrate (2-15 MBq) in 0.2 M sodium acetate buffer (100-300 µL, pH 4.0) at 80ºC for 15 minutes. The reaction solution was then purified by analytical RP-HPLC.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NOTA-RGD-BBN
radiochemical_method_description: The 68Ga labeling was performed according to methods previously described [3]. Briefly, 10 nmol of NOTA-RGD, NOTA-BBN or NOTA-RGD-BBN peptide was dissolved in 500 μl of 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer and incubated with 185 MBq of 68Ga for 10 min at 40°C

name: [99mTc]TcO(MAG2-3G3-dimer)
radiochemical_method_description: To a lyophilized vial containing 2.28 mg of NaH2PO4, 11.5 mg of Na2HPO4,50mgof α-D-glucoheptonic acid, 50 μgofSnCl2·2H2O, and25µlofthe MAG2-3G3-dimer conjugate was added 1.0 ml of Na99mTcO4 solution (10–50 mCi/ml). The vial was heated at 100°C for 20– 25 min in a lead-shielded water bath. After heating, the vial was placed back into the lead pig and allowed to stand at room temperature for ∼10 min.

name: [99mTc]TcTc–HYNIC–cHK
radiochemical_method_description: For 99mTc labeling, a mixture of 20 mg tricine (100 mg/mL in 25 mmol/L succinate buffer, pH 5.0), 740 MBq (20 mCi) Na99mTcO4, and 30 ll SnCl2 (1 lg/lL in 0.1 mol/L HCl) was successively added to 20 lg of HYNIC–cHK with constant stirring at 99 °C for 10 min. Then 100 ll of ethylenediamine-N,N0-diacetic acid (100 mg/mL) was added to the mixture with contant stirring at 99 °C for 20 min. After allowing it to cool down to room temperature, the mixture was purified with Sep-Pak C18 cartridges (Waters) as preciously described

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-R01-MG
radiochemical_method_description:
DOTA-
R01-MG was mixed with 68GaCl3 in sodium acetate buffer (pH
= 4.5) for 15 min at 90 °C. The mixture was purified by PD-10
column (GE Healthcare Life Sciences). Twelve 500 μL
samples were collected from the column. The active fraction
(usually 6 and 7) was then used for subsequent studies. The
purity of the product was analyzed by iTLC analysis.

name: iRGD-C6-lys(211At-ATE)-C6-DA7R
radiochemical_method_description: The labeling precursor (10 μL, 10 mg/mL in MeOH) was mixed with 211At (200 MBq, 50 μL) and N-iodosuccinimide (NIS, 5 μL, 3 mg/mL in MeOH with 1% acetic acid) in methanol. The mixture was then incubated at 37 °C for 10 min, and the labeling reaction was terminated by adding Na2S2O3 solution (5 μL, 20 mg/mL in water). The product was isolated by gel filtration on a SephadexTM G-10 column, with 0.1 mol L−1 phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.6) acting as the eluting solution. Quality control was performed using radio-TLC (thin layer chromatography), with acetone: water (v/v = 3:1) as the mobile phase (retention factor [Rf] iRGD-C6-lys(211At-ATE)-C6-DA7R = 0−0.1, [Rf] free 211At = 0.9−1). Radiolabeling of iRGD and DA7R using 211At was performed according to the same labeling method in the above experiment.

name: [67Ga]CG6
radiochemical_method_description: The NOTA-conjugated peptides (3-40 μg) were dissolved in 100 μL of 0.2 M sodium acetate buffer (pH 4.0), and the 67GaCl3 (2-87 MBq) was dissolved in 50 μL of 1 M HEPES buffer (pH 6.0). Both solutions were incubated at 75°C for 15 min.

name: [68Ga]Ga-Ac-CG6
radiochemical_method_description: The NOTA-conjugated peptides (5-20 μg) were dissolved in 80 μL of 1.5 M sodium acetate aqueous solution and mixed with 68GaCl3 (337-503 MBq) in 2.0 mL of 0.05 M HCl. The mixture was incubated at 75°C for 15 min and was then applied to Sep-Pak C18 Cartridges (Waters Corporation, Milford, MA).

name: [99mTc]Tc-isoDGR)
radiochemical_method_description: The 99mTc-isoDGR and 99mTc-3PisoDGR2 were prepared using a lyophilized kit with a labeling yield of >99%

name: [99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-PEG4-PisoDGR2
radiochemical_method_description: The 99mTc-isoDGR and 99mTc-3PisoDGR2 were prepared using a lyophilized kit with a labeling yield of >99%

name: [111I]In-DOTA-E-[c(RGDfK)]2
radiochemical_method_description: 250 μg of DOTA-E-[c(RGDfK)]2 was dissolved in 4.5 ml of 0.25 M ammoniumacetate buffer (pH 7.0), and 160 MBq of 111InCl3 (Mallinckrodt, Petten, the Netherlands) was added. The mixture was heated for 15 min at 100°C.

name: [99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-E-[c(RGDfK)]2
radiochemical_method_description: HYNIC-E-[c(RGDfK)]2 dissolved in 0.25 M succinate buffer (pH 5.0), added with tris(hydroxymethyl)methylglycine and triphenylphosphine-3,3',3"-trisulfonate, mixed with 99mTcO4-, heated at 100°C for 15 min. Quality control by silicagel instant thin layer chromatography and RP-HPLC with gradient elution.

name: [111In]In-DOTA-E-[c(RGDfK)]2
radiochemical_method_description: 250 μg of DOTA-E-[c(RGDfK)]2 was dissolved in 4.5 ml of 0.25 M ammoniumacetate buffer (pH 7.0), and 160 MBq of 111InCl3 (Mallinckrodt, Petten, the Netherlands) was added. The mixture was heated for 15 min at 100°C.

name: [18F]F-RWY
radiochemical_method_description: Briefly, we used lyophilized kits for rapid radiofluorination of NOTA-RWY peptide. The kits consisted of 8 nmol of NOTA-RWY and 6 nmol of AlCl3•6H2O. The reaction procedure was the following: the vials containing 8 nmol of NOTA-RWY and 6 nmol of AlCl3•6H2O was added with 65 μl of 18F- (about 10 mCi) in DI water, 3 μl of acetic acid and 324 μl acetonitrile, heated at 100 °C for 10 min. After the vial was cooled to room temperature,10 ml of water was added to dilute the mixture.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-RGD-GE11
radiochemical_method_description: [64Cu]CuCl2 was purchased from Dongcheng Advance Medical Sys tems Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. (Nanjing, China). The 64Cu-label ing of all conjugates were performed in 0.1 M ammonium acetate buffer (pH = 8.2) as follows: 6 μL of the conjugate stock solution (0.5 nmol/μL) was added to 2–3 mCi (74–111 MBq) of [64Cu]CuCl2 with 200 μL buffer and then heated at 90 ◦C for 30 min.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: [64Cu]CuCl2 was purchased from Dongcheng Advance Medical Sys tems Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. (Nanjing, China). The 64Cu-label ing of all conjugates were performed in 0.1 M ammonium acetate buffer (pH = 8.2) as follows: 6 μL of the conjugate stock solution (0.5 nmol/μL) was added to 2–3 mCi (74–111 MBq) of [64Cu]CuCl2 with 200 μL buffer and then heated at 90 ◦C for 30 min.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-GE11
radiochemical_method_description: [64Cu]CuCl2 was purchased from Dongcheng Advance Medical Sys tems Pharmaceutical Group Co., Ltd. (Nanjing, China). The 64Cu-label ing of all conjugates were performed in 0.1 M ammonium acetate buffer (pH = 8.2) as follows: 6 μL of the conjugate stock solution (0.5 nmol/μL) was added to 2–3 mCi (74–111 MBq) of [64Cu]CuCl2 with 200 μL buffer and then heated at 90 ◦C for 30 min.

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: Chelation with gallium-68 was performed by incubating 4.0 mL generator eluate (0.05 M HCl, 0.74−0.99 GBq) with a mixture of 10 nmol DOTA-precursor and 1.0 mL NaOAc (0.25 M) at 100 ◦C for 10 min. The mixture was diluted with 2.0 mL water and passed through a C18 cartridge. Subsequently, the C18 cartridge was washed with 5 mL water and eluted with a mixture of ethanol/H2O (v/v, 1 mL) to obtain the desired product. As regards lutetium-177 labelling, 74 MBq [177Lu] LuCl3 were diluted with 0.16 mL NaOAc (0.4 M, pH 5.5) and added to the precursor (28.0 nmol) in gentisic acid (2.0 mg). The reaction mixture was incubated at 95 ◦C for 15 min, then, it was purified by HPLC before its use in the experiments (Supplementary Fig. S8).

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-FAPI-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: Chelation with gallium-68 was performed by incubating 4.0 mL generator eluate (0.05 M HCl, 0.74−0.99 GBq) with a mixture of 10 nmol DOTA-precursor and 1.0 mL NaOAc (0.25 M) at 100 ◦C for 10 min. The mixture was diluted with 2.0 mL water and passed through a C18 cartridge. Subsequently, the C18 cartridge was washed with 5 mL water and eluted with a mixture of ethanol/H2O (v/v, 1 mL) to obtain the desired product. As regards lutetium-177 labelling, 74 MBq [177Lu] LuCl3 were diluted with 0.16 mL NaOAc (0.4 M, pH 5.5) and added to the precursor (28.0 nmol) in gentisic acid (2.0 mg). The reaction mixture was incubated at 95 ◦C for 15 min, then, it was purified by HPLC before its use in the experiments (Supplementary Fig. S8).

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-04
radiochemical_method_description: Chelation with gallium-68 was performed by incubating 4.0 mL generator eluate (0.05 M HCl, 0.74−0.99 GBq) with a mixture of 10 nmol DOTA-precursor and 1.0 mL NaOAc (0.25 M) at 100 ◦C for 10 min. As regards lutetium-177 labelling, 74 MBq [177Lu] LuCl3 were diluted with 0.16 mL NaOAc (0.4 M, pH 5.5) and added to the precursor (28.0 nmol) in gentisic acid (2.0 mg). The reaction mixture was incubated at 95 ◦C for 15 min, then, it was purified by HPLC before its use in the experiments (Supplementary Fig. S8).

name: [177Lu]Lu-AB-3PRGD2
radiochemical_method_description: No carrier-added 177LuCl3 for clinical use was purchased from ITM (Germany), with a specific activity of 3000 GBq/mg and a concentration of 36e44 GBq/mL. Typically, 3.7 GBq (100 mCi) 177LuCl3 was added to 200 mL of 0.5 mol/L NaOAc (pH 5.6), and the 177Lu dilution was injected into the vial with 100 mgof DOTAAB-3PRGD2 (Fig. 2A) provided by the Medical Isotopes Research Center, School of Basic Medical Sciences, Peking University. The mixture was heated in an upright position at 100 C for 30 min, then purified through a C18 cartridge and sterilized through a 0.22-mm aseptic filtration membrane.

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-HEVNPs
radiochemical_method_description: HEVNPs (0.13 mg/ml in 100 µl in PB) were diluted with metal-free 0.25 M ammonium acetate buffer (0.8 ml) in a reaction tube (5-ml Protein LoBind microtube, Eppendorf). This was followed by adding freshly eluted [68Ga]GaCl3 in 0.1 M HCl (80–140 MBq in 1 ml). The final reaction volume was 1.9 ml, and the reaction pH was 4.5. The reaction solution was mixed for 30 min at 63◦C with 350 rpm.

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-HEVNP-LXY30
radiochemical_method_description: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-HEVNP-LXY30 (700 µl in PB; 1.8-2.2 MBq) were added into 1.5-ml Protein LoBind microtubes containing 700 µl of PBS (0.01 M, pH 7.4), 100% human plasma, iron (FeCl3⋅ 6 H2O, 18 mM in PBS), or CO2-independent cell medium (from Gibco). All the samples (n = 3 per testing solution) were incubated at 37◦C. The amount of the intact 68Ga-radiolabeled HEVNP-LXY30 was monitored by radio-TLC at 5 min, 15 min, 30 min, and 60 min after the initiation of the incubation with the testing solutions.

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-2.5D
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA-2.5D or DOTA-2.5F (15 μg) was radiolabeled with 177Lu by addition of 74 MBq (2.0 mCi) 177LuCl3 in 0.2 M sodium acetate buffer (pH 5.0) followed by incubation for 0.5 h at 80°C.

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-2.5F
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA-2.5D or DOTA-2.5F (15 μg) was radiolabeled with 177Lu by addition of 74 MBq (2.0 mCi) 177LuCl3 in 0.2 M sodium acetate buffer (pH 5.0) followed by incubation for 0.5 h at 80°C.

name: [18F]AlF-NOTA-Rd2
radiochemical_method_description: [18F]fluoride was trapped on a QMA anion cartridge, followed by washing with 1 mL ofH2O and elution with 0.3 mL of 0.9% saline into the reaction vial. Further, 50 μL aliquot of 2 μg/μL NOTAPEG4-ZD2 was added to the reaction vial containing 6 μL of 2 mM AlCl3, 10 μL of HAc, and 1.5 mL of MeCN. The reaction mixture was heated at 100 °C for 15 min.

name: [18F]AlF-NOTA-A6P
radiochemical_method_description: [18F] fluoride (18.5–37 GBq) was trapped on a QMA anion cartridge and subsequently eluted with 0.5 mL 0.9 % saline into a reaction vial, containing 100 μg NOTA-A6P, 30 μl of 2 mM AlCl3, 5 μl of HAc, and 3.6 mL of DMSO, was heated at 100 ◦C for 15 min. Following cooling, the crude product was diluted with 10 mL of water and loaded onto a C18 column. The cartridge was then washed with water (20 mL) and eluted with 1.5 mL of 70 % ethanol, yielding the final product.

name: [99mTc]TcKeto-RGD-N4
radiochemical_method_description: Briefly, to a mixture of sodium tartrate (10 mg) and ammonium acetate (10 mg) in deionized water, 40 μLSnCl2 (2 mg/mL, dissolved in nitrogen-purged 0.1 M HCl) and (185–370) MBq sodium pertechnetate Na[99mTc]O4 was added. The reaction mixture was reached to 1 mL by normal saline and incubated at room temperature for 10 min. 200 μL of the peptide aqueous solution (0.1 μg/ μL) was added to the radiolabeled mixture and incubated at 50 °C for 30 min.

name: [99mTc]Tc-Nap-RGD-N4
radiochemical_method_description: Briefly, to a mixture of sodium tartrate (10 mg) and ammonium acetate (10 mg) in deionized water, 40 μLSnCl2 (2 mg/mL, dissolved in nitrogen-purged 0.1 M HCl) and (185–370) MBq sodium pertechnetate Na[99mTc]O4 was added. The reaction mixture was reached to 1 mL by normal saline and incubated at room temperature for 10 min. 200 μL of the peptide aqueous solution (0.1 μg/ μL) was added to the radiolabeled mixture and incubated at 50 °C for 30 min.

name: [68Ga]Ga-FAP-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: In brief, a solution of 25 µg DOTA-FAP-RGD in 1 mL 0.25M NaOAc buffer was added to 68GaCl3 solution eluted from an ITM [68Ge]Ge/[68Ga]Ga generator with 4 mL 0.05 M HCl. The final pH of the reaction mixture was determined to be 4.0–4.2. The reaction mixture was heated at 95 ℃ for 10 min.

name: [177Lu]Lu-FAP-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: 0.6 GBq of 177LuCl3 was diluted with 0.16 mL of NaOAc (0.4 M, pH 5.5) and added to precursor DOTA-FAP-RGD (20.0 nmol) with gentisic acid (2.0 mg). The reaction mixture was incubated at 95 ℃ for 15 min.

name: [68Ga]Ga-1
radiochemical_method_description: the pH of the generator eluate obtained as a 0.1 M HCl solution was adjusted to 3.5−4.0 by the addition of sodium acetate solution before 1−20 nmol of 1 or 2 was added and the reaction mixture was warmed to 45−50 °C for 10 min, yielding an almost quantitative incorporation of the 68Ga of ≥96%.

name: [68Ga]Ga-2
radiochemical_method_description: the pH of the generator eluate obtained as a 0.1 M HCl solution was adjusted to 3.5−4.0 by the addition of sodium acetate solution before 1−20 nmol of 1 or 2 was added and the reaction mixture was warmed to 45−50 °C for 10 min, yielding an almost quantitative incorporation of the 68Ga of ≥96%.

name: [68Ga]Ga-11
radiochemical_method_description: 11 and 12 were dissolved in water producing a stock solution of 1 μg μL−1.To20 μL of stock solution (i.e. 20 μgof 11 or 12) was added 200 μLof [68Ga]GaCl3 (0.1 M HCl; corresponding to ∼30 MBq 68Ga, to give a specific activity of ∼3 GBq μmol−1) and 40 μL of 2.0 M ammonium acetate (99.999% trace metals basis) the pH was checked to ensure it was in the range 6.5–7.5. The mixture was agitated and left at room temperature, before final analysis with HPLC-C18 and ITLC at 5 min and 35 min.

name: [68Ga]Ga-12
radiochemical_method_description: 11 and 12 were dissolved in water producing a stock solution of 1 μg μL−1.To20 μL of stock solution (i.e. 20 μgof 11 or 12) was added 200 μLof [68Ga]GaCl3 (0.1 M HCl; corresponding to ∼30 MBq 68Ga, to give a specific activity of ∼3 GBq μmol−1) and 40 μL of 2.0 M ammonium acetate (99.999% trace metals basis) the pH was checked to ensure it was in the range 6.5–7.5. The mixture was agitated and left at room temperature, before final analysis with HPLC-C18 and ITLC at 5 min and 35 min.

name: [99mTc]Tc-cKiE2
radiochemical_method_description: The 99mTc radiolabeling process of cKiE2 tracer was performed as following procedure. 1 mL of Na99mTcO4 solution (~370 MBq) was added to a lyophilized kit containing tricine (6.5 mg), trisodium triphenylphosphine-3,3’,3’’-trisulfonate (TPPTS, 5 mg), succinic acid (59 mg), sodium hydroxide (20 mg), and HYNIC-cKiE2 (50 μg). 100 °C for 20-25 min.

name: [111In]In-β-DA6
radiochemical_method_description: Radioactive labeling is carried out by incubating the precursor with [111In]InCl3 in 2-(N-morpholino)ethanesulfonic acid (MES) buffer (pH=5.6)

name: [111In]In-RGd-DA1
radiochemical_method_description: Radioactive labeling is carried out by incubating the precursor with [111In]InCl3 in 2-(N-morpholino)ethanesulfonic acid (MES) buffer (pH=5.6)

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-PEG-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA-PEG-RGD was labeled with 64Cu by the addition of 37–185 MBq (1–5 mCi) of 64Cu (5–25 µg of DOTA-PEG-RGD per 37 MBq of 64Cu) in 0.1N sodium acetate (pH 5.5) buffer and 45 min of incubation at 50°C. The reaction was terminated by the addition of 5 µL of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid solution (10 mmol/L).

name: [64Cu]-FIB504
radiochemical_method_description: to 2 mCi 64Cu in 5-10 μL HCl (0.04 N) was added approximately 3 volume equivalents of pH 5, 0.1 M acetate buffer. To this was added the antibody (e.g. 250 μg, 75 μL), also in acetate buffer. name: [64Cu]DOTA-ADIBON3-AlaPEG28-A20FMDV2
radiochemical_method_description:
Pre-click approach A:Aqueous ammonium acetate buffer solution (0.1 M, pH 5, 25 lL) was added to the [64Cu]Cl2 solution (1 mCi). The peptide conjugate 3 (25 lg, 5.8 nmol) in water (25 lL) was then added to the solution (final pH 5). The reaction mixture was incubated for 15 min at 37 °C and subsequently quenched by adding ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA) solution (100 lL, 5 mM) to complex any free [64Cu].
Post-click approach B:Aqueous ammonium acetate (0.1 M, pH 5, 25 lL) buffer solution was added to the [64Cu]Cl2 solution (1 mCi). The pH of the final solution was 5. Compound 2 (25 lg, 35.4 nmol) in water (25 lL) was then added to the solution (final pH 5). The reaction mixture was incubated for 15 min at 37 °C and aliquots directly analyzed by HPLC. Without any purification the click reaction was carried out by adding 1.37 eq peptide 1 (171 lg, 47.9 nmol, 17 lL water) at room temperature or 37 °C.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NODAGA-c(RGDfK)
radiochemical_method_description: Using a 1110 MBq ⁶⁸Ge/⁶⁸Ga generator, elute with 7 mL 0.1N HCl to collect ⁶⁸Ga. The eluted ⁶⁸Ga solution (240-290 MBq) is then loaded onto a cation-exchange column (Strata-XC; Phenomenex). Elute the column with 800 μL acetone/HCl mixture (97.6% v/v, 0.02N), collecting the eluate directly into a tube containing 2 mL 0.2 mol/L sodium acetate buffer (pH 4.0) and 10 μg NODAGA-c(RGDfK).

name: [64Cu]Cu-NODAGA-c(RGDfK)
radiochemical_method_description: Using ⁶⁴CuCl₂ (37–74 MBq) and 5–10 μg NODAGA-c(RGDfK) in 0.1 mol/L ammonium acetate buffer (pH 8.0), labeling was completed in 10 min at room temperature without heating. Post-labeling, radiochemical purity (>97%) was verified via RP-HPLC, specific activity (15–20 GBq/μmol) determined, and the product diluted with 0.9% NaCl for use.

name: [64Cu]Cu-CB-TE2A-c(RGDfK)
radiochemical_method_description: Using ⁶⁴CuCl₂ (37–74 MBq) and 5–10 μg CB-TE2A-c(RGDfK) in 0.1 mol/L ammonium acetate buffer (pH 8.0), labeling was performed at 95 °C for 30 min. Post-reaction, radiochemical purity (>97%) and specific activity (15–20 GBq/μmol) were confirmed by RP-HPLC. The product was diluted with 0.9% NaCl for experimental use.

name: [⁹⁹ᵐTc]Tc(CO)3-ADA-PIA
radiochemical_method_description: Using 99mTc(CO)3​(H2​O)3​+ precursor and PIA-ADA conjugate in sodium acetate buffer (pH 4.5), labeling was performed at 70°C for 45 minutes. The resulting 99mTc(CO)3​-ADA-PIA complex was purified using sequential Sep-Pak C-18 and QMA cartridges, with radiochemical purity (>95%) confirmed by reverse-phase HPLC. The final product was formulated in phosphate buffer.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NODAGA-RGDyK
radiochemical_method_description: The ⁶⁸Ga eluate was obtained using 0.1 mol/L HCl from a ⁶⁸Ge/⁶⁸Ga generator (IGG100), and the high-activity fraction was collected. 20 μg of NODAGA-RGDyK was added to the ⁶⁸Ga fraction and reacted for 20 min at room temperature in the Modular-Lab PharmTracer system. The product was purified via an integrated cartridge, eluted with 50% ethanol, filtered through a 0.22 μm membrane, and diluted in saline.

name: [¹⁸⁸Re]Re-IDA-D-[c(RGDfK)]₂
radiochemical_method_description: IDA-D-[c(RGDfK)]₂ (0.3 mg) was dissolved in 300 μL ultrapure water. [¹⁸⁸Re(H₂O)₃(CO)₃]⁺ (50–100 mCi) was added and reacted at 75°C for 60 min. Semi-preparative HPLC (C18 column, CH₃CN/H₂O/TFA gradient elution) gave a retention time of 19.2 min.

name: [⁶⁸Ga]Ga-DOTA-E{E[c(RGDfK)]₂}₂
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁸GaCl₃ was eluted from a TiO₂ generator with 0.1M HCl. The eluate (315-365 MBq) was reacted with DOTA-RGD in ammonium acetate/HEPES buffer at 95°C for 20 min. The product was purified on an Oasis HLB column, washed with water, and eluted with 25% ethanol. The final product was diluted in PBS to achieve <5% ethanol.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NOPO−c(RGDfK)
radiochemical_method_description: Use the eluate from a ⁶⁸Ge/⁶⁸Ga generator (1.0 M HCl) directly without pre-purification. Mix a trace amount of precursor (0.1-1 nmol) with HEPES buffer, add the ⁶⁸Ga eluate, and heat at 100°C for 5 min for labeling. Purify the reaction mixture using a C18 solid-phase extraction cartridge.

name: [64Cu]Cu-CB-TE1A1P-LLP2A
radiochemical_method_description: Labeling was performed by incubating with CB-TE1A1P-LLP2A in 0.1 M ammonium acetate buffer (pH 8.1) at 90 °C for 5 minutes using ⁶⁴Cu, followed by purification and analysis via radio-HPLC on a C18 column.

name: [⁹⁹ᵐTc]Tc-RGD-Lys-(Arg¹¹) CCMSH
radiochemical_method_description: Using ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻ (37–74 MBq) and 10 μg of RGD-Lys-(Arg¹¹)CCMSH peptide in a glucoheptonate transchelation system, labeling was achieved by incubation at 75°C for 40 min at pH 8.5. Post-labeling, the product was purified via RP-HPLC to achieve high radiochemical purity (>98%) was determined. The final product was diluted with normal saline (pH adjusted to 5.0).

name: [⁹⁹ᵐTc]Tc-EDDA₂/HYNIC-IAC
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁹ᵐTc was labeled with IAC-HYNIC using SnCl₂ reduction in tricine/succinate buffer (pH 6, 37°C, 30 min). EDDA was then added and the complex was heated (80°C, 25 min). The product was purified on a C-18 cartridge with 20% ACN, confirmed by HPLC, and formulated in PBS for biological studies.

name: [64Cu]Cu-cyclam-RAFT-c(-RGDfK-)4
radiochemical_method_description: The precursor cyclam-RAFT-c(-RGDfK-)₄ was dissolved in 10% HEPES/90% MeOH (10 mM, pH 7.0) or DMSO for storage. ⁶⁴CuCl₂ was reconstituted in 100 mM citrate buffer (pH 5.5). Equal volumes of 0.4 mM precursor and 1.18 MBq/μL ⁶⁴CuCl₂ were mixed and incubated at 37 °C for 60 min. Labeling efficiency was determined by RP-HPLC or TLC with autoradiography.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-3-4A
radiochemical_method_description: Add ⁶⁴CuCl₂ solution to NOTA-3-4A and incubate at 37°C for 1 hour. Purify the labeled product using a PD-10 column (GE Healthcare Life Sciences) with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.4) as the eluent to remove unbound free ⁶⁴Cu and other impurities, obtaining high-purity ⁶⁴Cu-NOTA-3-4A probe.

name: [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-DOTA-E-c (RGDfK)₂
radiochemical_method_description: Take 5 ml of DOTA-E-c(RGDfK)₂ (1 mg/ml) and dilute it with 50 ml of 1M pH 5 acetate buffer.Add 7 μl of ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ solution.Incubate at 90°C for 30 minutes, then dilute to 4 ml with injectable-grade water.

name: [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-AuNP-c (RGDfK) C
radiochemical_method_description: Add 0.025 ml of 5 mM c(RGDfK)C to 1 ml of 20 nm AuNP solution (each 20 nm AuNP binds 108 molecules).Add 3 ml of ¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTA-GGC (0.25 μg peptide, 1.89×10¹⁴ molecules, each 20 nm AuNP binds 270 molecules).Stir for 5 minutes to form the multimer system without further purification.

name: [⁶⁸Ga]Ga-DOTA-RGD peptide
radiochemical_method_description: Elute ⁶⁸GaCl₃ from a ⁶⁸Ge/⁶⁸Ga generator (loaded with 1850 MBq ⁶⁸Ge) using 0.1 M HCl; adjust pH to 4.4 (with HEPES), then incubate with DOTA-RGD peptide (13 nmol) at 100°C for 20 minutes; determine radiochemical purity by radio-HPLC (μBondapak C18 column, mobile phase containing TFA/acetonitrile/phosphate).

name: [64Cu]Cu-CB-TE2A-c(RGDyK)
radiochemical_method_description: Add 64CuCl₂ to CB-TE2A-c(RGDyK) dissolved in 0.1 M ammonium acetate buffer (pH=8), and heat the mixture at 95°C for 1 hour; monitor the reaction by radio-HPLC and radio-TLC.

name: [⁹⁰Y]Y-DOTA-c (RGDfK)
radiochemical_method_description: Mix ⁹⁰YCl₃ with DOTA-c(RGDfK) in 3M acetic acid buffer (pH 6.0), heat at 100°C for 15 minutes to achieve labeling, followed by purification using reversed-phase high-performance liquid chromatography (RP-HPLC, Cosmosil 5C₁₈-AR 300 column)

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-bitistatin
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA was activated with EDC/sulfo-NHS and conjugated to bitistatin. The conjugate was then labeled with ⁶⁴CuCl₂ in 0.03 M citrate buffer (pH 6.5) at 55°C for 40 minutes. After purification, the specific activity was approximately 250 µCi/µg, with a radiochemical purity of about 96% as determined by FPLC.

name: [64Cu]Cu-BAD-2IT-Bitistatin
radiochemical_method_description: BAD and 2-iminothiolane (2IT) were used to co-modify bitistatin. The product was then reacted with ⁶⁴CuCl₂ in 0.1 M citrate buffer (pH 6.5) at 55°C for 40 minutes. After purification, the specific activity reached 50 µCi/µg, with a radiochemical purity of 90% as determined by FPLC.

name: [⁹⁹ᵐTc]Tc-RAFT-RGD
radiochemical_method_description: Using the IsoLink kit, ⁹⁹ᵐTc-RAFT-RGD was heated at 100°C for 20 min. The pH was adjusted to 7-8 with 2N HCl, followed by adding 100μg of ligand and incubating at 60°C for 20 min. After cooling for 20 min at room temperature, radiochemical purity (RCP) was determined by TLC. Stability was assessed by HPLC.

name: [18F]F-FB-PEG3-Glu-RGD-BBN
radiochemical_method_description: [¹⁸F]-SFB was synthesized and purified as reported, dried, and redissolved in DMSO. The [¹⁸F]-FB-PEG₃-Glu-RGD-BBN fraction was collected, dried, and reconstituted in PBS.

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-E{E[c(RGDfK)]2}2
radiochemical_method_description: 64CuCl₂ diluted in 0.1 mol/L sodium acetate and added to DOTA conjugate (5 μg peptide per mCi 64Cu). Incubated for 1 h at 50°C. Purified by semipreparative HPLC.

name: [⁹⁹ᵐTc]Tc-T7
radiochemical_method_description: T7 peptide was labeled via carbonyl technetium. A vial containing sodium borohydride, carbonate, and tartrate was purged with CO, then reacted with ⁹⁹mTcO₄⁻ at 80°C. After cooling and pH adjustment, the resulting ⁹⁹mTc(CO)₃⁺ intermediate was reacted with T7 at 50°C. The final product was purified by HPLC.

radiochemical_method_description: AHN-12 reacts with 20-40x molar excess 1B4M-DTPA in pH 8.6 carbonate buffer overnight, purified, labeled with ⁹⁰YCl₃, quenched with EDTA, and verified by ITLC-SG.

radiochemical_method_description: AHN-12 reacts with 20-40x molar excess 1B4M-DTPA in pH 8.6 carbonate buffer overnight (RT), purified via Centricon filter, labeled with ¹¹¹InCl₃, quenched with EDTA, and verified by ITLC-SG.

name: [²¹¹At]-211-anti-CD45 monoclonal antibody (mAb)​​
radiochemical_method_description: ²¹¹At is connected to an anti-CD45 antibody via a conjugated closo-decaborate(2-) derivative (B10), and B10 is coupled to the antibody through lysine amines.

name: [¹⁷⁷Lu]-177-DOTA-PEG4-LLP2A
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA-PEG4-LLP2A reacts with ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ under appropriate conditions, is labeled using the chloramine-T method, and then purified through a PD-10 column.

name: [89Zr]Zr-Df-CD45
radiochemical_method_description: The antibody reacts with Df-p-SCN in pH 9 buffer for 1-2 hours (40°C). After purification, it is incubated with ⁸⁹Zr in pH 7 buffer for 1-2 hours (room temperature). EDTA is added to remove unchelated ⁸⁹Zr, and finally, purification is performed using size exclusion chromatography.

name: [⁹⁹ᵐTc]-99m-anti-NCA-95 monoclonal antibody
radiochemical_method_description: Reduce antibodies with Tris-(2 - carboxyethyl)phosphine for 20 min at RT under N₂, purify, incubate with ⁹⁹ᵐTc or ¹⁸⁸Re for 2 h at 37°C, and perform QC by HPLC and ITLC.

name: [¹⁸⁸Re]-188-anti-NCA-95 monoclonal antibody
radiochemical_method_description: Reduce antibodies with Tris-(2 - carboxyethyl)phosphine for 20 min at RT under N₂, purify, incubate with ⁹⁹ᵐTc or ¹⁸⁸Re for 2 h at 37°C, and perform QC by HPLC and ITLC.

name: 90Y-DOTA-30F11
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA-antibody conjugates chelate with ⁹⁰Y or ¹⁷⁷Lu under metal-free conditions, and purification is performed using size exclusion chromatography (PD10 column).

name: [225 Ac]Actinium-225-Lintuzumab
radiochemical_method_description: conjugated to a variety of antibodies using DOTA-SCN

name: ¹⁸⁸Re-抗CD66 mAb
radiochemical_method_description: After being reduced with tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP), the antibody reacts with ¹⁸⁸Re-perrhenate in a phthalate buffer (pH 5.6) in the presence of tin ions and methylenediphosphonate.

name: ⁶⁸Ga-IMP288
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁸Ga-IMP-288 was synthesized by labeling 3.4 nmol IMP-288 with the second 1 mL generator eluate fraction (380 MBq) in 1.0 M HEPES buffer (pH 7.0) at 95°C for 20 min. The product was purified via an Oasis HLB column after EDTA quenching, with final elution using 25% ethanol.

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-cMORF
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁹ᵐTc labeling was performed in two steps: S-acetyl NHS-MAG₃ was conjugated to cMORF in HEPES buffer (pH 8.0, ≥1 h), purified by P4 column (ammonium acetate, pH 5.2), and frozen. The conjugate was then labeled with ⁹⁹ᵐTc using tartrate and SnCl₂/ascorbate (pH 9.2, boiling, 20 min), followed by final P4 purification (phosphate buffer, pH 7.2).

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-anti-CEA pIgA-SH
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁹ᵐTc labeling of anti-CEA pIgA involved thiolation with 2-IT (1.7±0.4 -SH/IgA), purification, and reaction with a kit-synthesized ⁹⁹ᵐTc-tricarbonyl precursor (2220–3700 MBq) at 100°C. The neutralized precursor (148–185 MBq, pH 7.0) was incubated with thiolated IgA at 25°C for 120 min. ITLC confirmed labeling (Rf=1.0), allowing direct use without purification.

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTA-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-labeled DOTA-M5A was prepared by conjugating M5A with NHS-DOTA, purifying via 30 kDa Amicon into ammonium acetate (pH 7.0), and confirming molecular weight (≈150 kDa) by MS. Labeling (15 MBq ¹⁷⁷Lu : 50 μg conjugate) proceeded in acetate/ammonium buffer (pH 5.5) at 42°C, 350 rpm, 1 h, with RCP assessed by ITLC and HPLC-SEC, and purified if needed.

name: ⁶⁸Ga-Ga-HNI01
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁸Ga-THP-HNI01 was prepared by conjugating HNI01-Cys with 10-fold mal-THP (pH 7.4, 37°C, 1 h), purified via PD-10 and verified by LC-MS/SDS-PAGE. ⁶⁸Ga (eluted in 0.05 M HCl) was pH-adjusted with sodium acetate, then labeled with 150–200 μg conjugate in PBS (RT, 10 min).

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-6B11
radiochemical_method_description: The [⁹⁹ᵐTc(CO)₃]⁺ precursor was synthesized from ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻ (370–740 MBq) using an IsoLink® kit (100°C, 25 min), with purity verified by TLC (Rf=0.0, free Tc <2%). For labeling, purified 6B11 VHH (∼5 nmol) was reacted with the neutralized precursor (∼65 MBq) at room temperature for 30 min, followed by HPLC purification and TLC confirmation (Rf=1.0 for conjugate).

name: ⁸⁹Zr-Df-IAB22M2C
radiochemical_method_description: ⁸⁹Zr-labeled Df-IAB22M2C was prepared by conjugating IAB22M2C with a 10-fold molar excess of Df-NCO in pH 8.0 bicarbonate buffer (2 h, RT), purified by SEC-HPLC (80 kDa fraction). ⁸⁹Zr labeling (10–50 MBq, pH-adjusted) was performed with the conjugate (10 μg/μL) at 37°C for 1 h, followed by radio-HPLC and TLC validation (RCP >95%, Rf=0.8 for labeled conjugate).

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-scFv-96NRT
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-scFv-96NRT was prepared by reducing 0.5 mg scFv with SnCl₂ in PBS (5 min, RT) to expose thiol groups, followed by labeling with 370 MBq ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻ in the presence of glucoheptonate (30 min, RT). The product was purified via PD-10 column, and radiochemical purity (RCP >95%) was confirmed by radio-TLC (free ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻ Rf=1.0, labeled conjugate Rf=0.0).

name: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-hT84.66-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-hT84.66-M5A was prepared by conjugating hT84.66-M5A (5 mg) with 10-fold DOTA-NHS in PBS (2 h, RT), purified via PD-10 column (1-2 DOTAs/Ab, >95% purity). ⁶⁴Cu labeling (1080–1402 MBq, pH-adjusted to 5.5–6.0) proceeded with the conjugate at 43°C for 45 min, followed by HPLC purification (IgG-retention peak), sterile filtration, and formulation in saline with 1% HSA for clinical use.

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-scFv-Fc
radiochemical_method_description: 2 mg scFv-Fc was reacted with a 1000:1 molar ratio of DOTA-NHS in pH 7.0 buffer at room temperature for 18–24 hours, purified by dialysis against 0.2 M NH₄OAc and concentrated to >5 mg/mL. 400 μg DOTA-scFv-Fc was incubated with 1.3 mCi ¹¹¹InCl₃ in 0.25 M NH₄OAc (pH 5.0) at 43°C for 1 hour, followed by size-exclusion HPLC purification of monomers and radiochemical purity validation.

name: ¹⁵³Sm-DB₂
radiochemical_method_description: A 10 μL aliquot of DB₂ solution (2 g/L) was reacted with 37 MBq of ¹⁵³SmCl₃ at room temperature for 20 minutes. The reaction was verified by TLC using 85% methanol as the mobile phase.

name: ⁶⁸Ga-IMP-288
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁸Ga was eluted in 0.1 M HCl (2nd 1 mL fraction, 380 MBq) and labeled with 3.4 nmol IMP-288 in HEPES buffer (pH 7.0) at 95°C for 20 min. After EDTA quenching, HLB purification (water wash, 25% EtOH elution) gave >95% RCP product.

name: ¹¹¹In-IMP156
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-labeled IMP156 was prepared by reconstituting 11 μg lyophilized peptide with 1 mL saline, then reacting 15 μL (0.11 μg) with 1.5 mCi ¹¹¹InCl₃ in 65 μL ammonium acetate buffer (pH 5.4) at room temperature for 60 minutes. After confirming RCP >95% by instant TLC (silica gel, methanol/water=55:45 or citrate buffer pH 6.0), a 3-fold molar excess of InCl₃ was added to saturate DTPA and prevent metal interference.

name: ⁹⁰Y-IMP-241
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁰Y-labeled DOTA-IMP-241 was prepared by incubating pre-conjugated DOTA-bisHSG peptide (0.305–0.427 nmol) with ⁹⁰YCl₃ (500–900 μCi) in ammonium acetate buffer (pH 5.0, 30 min, RT). RCP >97% was confirmed by instant TLC (citrate buffer pH 6.0), and no further purification was needed as >97% radioactivity transferred to hBS14 after incubation.

name: ⁸⁹Zr-Bevacizuma
radiochemical_method_description: ⁸⁹Zr-DOTA-bevacizumab was prepared by conjugating bevacizumab (25 mg/mL) with a 50-fold molar excess of DOTA in sodium bicarbonate buffer (pH 8.5, 1h, RT), followed by labeling with 6.9±4.0 MBq ⁸⁹ZrCl₃ in ammonium acetate buffer (pH 6.8, 37°C, 2h). The product was pre-filtered (0.22 μm) with endotoxin <1 EU/mL.

name: ¹⁸⁸Re-AG 8.0
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁸⁸Re-AG 8.0 was prepared by first synthesizing ¹⁸⁸Re-glucoheptonate (740 MBq, N₂, 1h RT, >80% purity) and deacetylating AG 8.0 with NaOH to expose free thiols. Ligand exchange labeling (21 nmol deacetylated AG 8.0, 100°C, 15 min) was followed by SepPak C₁₈ purification (TFA to remove free ¹⁸⁸Re, PBS/ethanol to elute conjugate), yielding RCP >90% with confirmed immunoreactivity.

name: ¹¹¹In-DTPA-F33-104
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DTPA-F33-104 was prepared by conjugating F33-104 with DTPA anhydride (1.8–2.1 DTPAs/antibody) in DMSO (4°C, 12h), purified via Sephadex G25 (NaHCO₃ elution). Labeling with 74 MBq ¹¹¹InCl₃ in acetate buffer (pH 5.8, 60min, RT) was followed by Na₂EDTA quenching and Sephadex G25 purification.

name: ⁹⁰Y-DTPA-F33-104
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁰Y-DTPA-F33-104 was prepared by conjugating F33-104 with DTPA anhydride (1.8–2.1 DTPAs/antibody) in DMSO (4°C, 12h), purified via Sephadex G25 (NaHCO₃ elution). Labeling with 925 MBq ⁹⁰Y-acetate (pretreated in acetate buffer pH 5.8) proceeded at RT for 60 min.

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-MAG₃-cMORF
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-MORF/cMORF was prepared by conjugating amine-modified MORF/cMORF with S-acetyl NHS-MAG₃ in HEPES buffer (pH 8.0, 1h RT), purified via BioGel P-4 (ammonium acetate pH 5.2). Labeling with 1–2 mCi ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻ in tartrate/stannous chloride solution (20min boiling) was followed by BioGel P-4 purification (PBS pH 7.2) and HPLC purity verification.

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-T84.66 Diabody
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-Diabody was prepared by conjugating 400 μg diabody with DOTA (1:1000 molar ratio) in NH₄OAc buffer (pH 7.0, 4°C, 18–24h, 0.5 DOTA/antibody), dialyzed and concentrated. Labeling with 1.3 mCi ¹¹¹InCl₃ (NH₄OAc pH 5.0, 43°C, 1h) was quenched with DTPA, followed by Superdex 75 purification (PBS pH 7.2) and HPLC purity verification.

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-T84.66 Minibody
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-Minibody was prepared by conjugating 400 μg minibody with DOTA (1:1000 molar ratio, 1–6 DOTA/antibody) in NH₄OAc buffer (pH 7.0, 4°C, 18–24h), dialyzed and concentrated. Labeling with 1.3 mCi ¹¹¹InCl₃ (NH₄OAc pH 5.0, 43°C, 1h) was quenched with DTPA, followed by TSK-G2000SW purification (PBS pH 7.2) and HPLC purity verification.

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-IMP-192
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-IMP-192 was prepared from a lyophilized kit (78 μg IMP-192, 100 μg Sn(II), ascorbate, 6× cold In) by adding 40 mCi ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻ saline, incubating at RT for 10 min, then heating in boiling water for 15 min. No purification was needed, with RCP 94–99% by RP-HPLC (C₁₈, 0.1% TFA/ACN), free ⁹⁹ᵐTc <1% by ITLC, and >94% binding to bsMAb by SE-HPLC.

name: ¹⁸⁸Re-IMP-192
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁸⁸Re-IMP-192 was prepared from a lyophilized kit (50 μg IMP-192, 660 μg Sn(II), ascorbate, 6× cold In) by adding 17 mCi ¹⁸⁸ReO₄⁻ saline, heating in boiling water for 1 hour. No purification was needed, with RCP 96–97% by RP-HPLC, free ¹⁸⁸Re <3% by ITLC, and >96% binding to bsMAb by SE-HPLC.

name: ¹¹¹In-VSC-DOTA-chT84.66
radiochemical_method_description: VSC-DOTA was synthesized via Michael addition of cysteinyl-DOTA to vinyl sulfone (pH 7.5, DTT reduction), purified by ion exchange (formic acid gradient), and lyophilized (626.7 Da). chT84.66 was partially reduced (TCEP) and conjugated with 50× VSC-DOTA under argon. ¹¹¹In labeling (0.3 mCi/mg, 43°C, 60 min) included DTPA quenching and size-exclusion purification.

name: ¹¹¹In-[Lys (DTPA)]-NT
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DTPA-NT was prepared by reacting DTPA dianhydride with the lysine ε-NH₂ of NT in HEPES/DMSO (pH 8.2), purified by C₁₈ HPLC (>95% purity, ESI-MS: calc. 2048.32, obs. 2048.16). Labeling with 11.1 MBq ¹¹¹InCl₃ in acetate-citrate buffer (pH 5.0, 22h RT) was followed by cold InCl₃ saturation and Sep-Pak purification.

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-GLGK [Nᵋ-(HVS)₂]-Cys-diabody
radiochemical_method_description: The preparation involved engineering the anti-CEA diabody to introduce a C-terminal cysteine, expressing and purifying it, then conjugating the purified protein with a pre-synthesized DOTA-GLGK-[Nᵋ-(HVS)₂] bifunctional chelator. The resulting conjugate was finally radiolabeled with ¹¹¹In/⁶⁴Cu and purified for use.

name: ⁹⁹ᵐTc-IMP-192
radiochemical_method_description: IMP-192, an octapeptide with dual DTPA and TscGC motifs, was synthesized by SPPS. After purification and saturation of DTPA sites with cold InCl₃, it was lyophilized with Sn(II) and ascorbate into a single-vial kit. For ⁹⁹ᵐTc-labeling, the kit was reconstituted with ⁹⁹ᵐTcO₄⁻, incubated at room temperature, and then heated in a boiling water bath to facilitate chelation, with RCP verified by ITLC.

name: ¹¹¹In-cys-DOTA-PEG3400-diabody
radiochemical_method_description: The anti-CEA diabody was conjugated first with NHS-PEG3400-VS and then with cys-DOTA through a two-step reaction, each followed by purification. The resulting cys-DOTA-PEG3400-diabody was labeled with ¹¹¹In at 43°C for 60 min, and the final product was purified by size-exclusion chromatography.

name: ¹¹¹In-SH-DO3A-VS-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: The disulfide bonds of M5A were reduced with TCEP and then conjugated to DO3A-VS. After purification, the conjugate was labeled with ¹¹¹InCl₃ in ammonium acetate buffer (pH 6.5) at 43°C for 45-60 minutes. The final product was purified by size-exclusion chromatography following DTPA quenching.

name: ⁶⁴Cu-NH-DO3A-VS-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: he M5A antibody was first conjugated to DO3A-VS via amine coupling at pH 9.0. The resulting conjugate was then labeled with ⁶⁴CuCl₂ in citrate buffer (pH 5.5) at 43°C for 45 minutes, followed by purification with a size-exclusion column after DTPA quenching.

name: [¹⁸F] AlF-NODA-MPAEM-hMN-14-diabody
radiochemical_method_description: The NODA-MPAEM chelator was synthesized and then labeled with ¹⁸F via [¹⁸F]AlF complexation to form [¹⁸F]AlF-NODA-MPAEM with high efficiency. The resulting radiolabeled chelator was subsequently conjugated to a diabody fragment at room temperature in 20 minutes, and the final radioimmunoconjugate was purified by size-exclusion chromatography.

name: [¹⁸F] AlF-NODA-MPAEM-hMN-14-Fab′
radiochemical_method_description: The NODA-MPAEM chelator was synthesized and purified by HPLC, then labeled with ¹⁸F via [¹⁸F]AlF complexation at 106°C (94±2% efficiency). The resulting [¹⁸F]AlF-NODA-MPAEM was conjugated to Fab′ fragments at room temperature for 20 minutes (70-77% efficiency), followed by PD-10 column purification.

name: [¹⁸F] AlF-NODA-MPAEM-hMN-14-Fab-AD2
radiochemical_method_description: The NODA-MPAEM chelator was synthesized, purified, and then efficiently labeled with ¹⁸F via [¹⁸F]AlF complexation (94±2% yield). The resulting [¹⁸F]AlF-NODA-MPAEM was subsequently conjugated to Fab-AD2 fragments at room temperature, and the final radioimmunoconjugate was purified by size-exclusion chromatography.

name: ⁶⁴Cu-F6-te1pa
radiochemical_method_description: The F6 antibody was conjugated with te1pa at a 700:1 molar ratio on ice for 3 hours. After purification, the conjugate was labeled with ⁶⁴Cu at a 1.5:1 (antibody:⁶⁴Cu) ratio in ammonium acetate buffer at 40°C for 25 minutes, and the labeling efficiency was verified by ITLC.

name: ⁶⁴Cu-F6-C-dota
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁴Cu-F6-te1pa/F6-C-dota was prepared by conjugating F6 antibody with p-NCS-Bn-dota (15:1 molar ratio) in carbonate buffer (pH 8.6, 12h RT), purified by PD-10 (5% aggregates). Labeling with ⁶⁴CuCl₂ (25.1 GBq/μmol) in ammonium acetate (pH 6.5–7, 40°C, 25min) at 1.5:1 antibody-to-Cu ratio was quenched with EDTA, and ITLC confirmed labeling efficiency (free ⁶⁴Cu Rf=1.0, bound Rf=0).

name: ⁹⁰Y-mAb C2-45
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁰Y-DTPA-C2-45 was prepared by conjugating mAb C2-45 with SCN-Bn-DTPA (5:1 molar ratio) in borate buffer (pH 8.5, 37°C, 20h), purified by Bio-Spin 6 (2.0 DTPAs/antibody). ⁹⁰Y labeling (1 mg conjugate in acetate buffer pH 5.5, 37°C, 1h) was purified via PD-10 and verified by ITLC (free ⁹⁰Y Rf=1.0).

name: ¹¹¹In-mAb C2-45
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DTPA-C2-45 was prepared by conjugating mAb C2-45 with SCN-Bn-DTPA (5:1 molar ratio) in borate buffer (pH 8.5, 37°C, 20h), purified by Bio-Spin 6 (2.0 DTPAs/antibody). Labeling with ¹¹¹InCl₃ in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, RT, 1h) was followed by Bio-Spin 6 purification.

name: ⁹⁰Y-DOTA-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: ⁹⁰Y-DOTA-M5A was prepared under cGMP by conjugating humanized M5A IgG₁ with DOTA-NHS (5:1 molar ratio) in borate buffer (pH 8.5, 20h RT), purified by Superdex 75. Labeling with ⁹⁰YCl₃ (e.g., 10 mCi/m²) in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, 37°C, 1h) was followed by PD-10 purification and ITLC verification (free ⁹⁰Y Rf=1.0).

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-M5A was prepared under cGMP by conjugating humanized M5A IgG₁ with DOTA-NHS (5:1 molar ratio) in borate buffer (pH 8.5, 20h RT), purified by Superdex 75. Labeling with 5 mCi ¹¹¹InCl₃ in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, RT, 1h) was followed by PD-10 purification and ITLC verification.

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-T84.66-scFv-HSA
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-T84.66-scFv-HSA was produced by fusing T84.66-scFv with truncated HSA, expressed in NS0 cells (clone 17F9), and purified by Ni²⁺/hydroxyapatite chromatography. Conjugation with DOTA-NHS (1:17 molar ratio, >90% efficiency) was followed by ¹¹¹In labeling (0.88 mCi ¹¹¹InCl₃, pH 5.5, 43°C, 30min), DTPA quenching, and Superdex 75 purification.

name: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-T84.66-scFv-HSA
radiochemical_method_description: ¹²⁵I-T84.66-scFv-HSA was produced by fusing T84.66-scFv (GS18 linker) with truncated HSA via overlap PCR, expressed in NS0 cells (clone 17F9: 5–45 μg/mL), and purified by Ni²⁺ affinity and ceramic hydroxyapatite chromatography (>95% purity). Radiolabeling used Iodogen method (20 μg Iodogen, 1.2 mCi ¹²⁵I-NaI, pH 7.5, 3min RT), followed by Superdex 75 purification.

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-IMP288
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTA-IMP288 was labeled from IMP288 (dual-HSG peptide) using ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ in ammonium acetate buffer (pH 5.5, 37°C, 30min). The TF2 bsAb (anti-CEACAM5/anti-HSG trivalent fusion) was produced via DNL and Protein A purification. Radiolabeling was verified by TLC (free ¹⁷⁷Lu Rf=1.0) and RP-HPLC.

name: ⁶⁸Ga-IMP288
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁸Ga-IMP288 was labeled by reacting generator-eluted ⁶⁸Ga³⁺ with IMP288 (dual-HSG peptide) in ammonium acetate (pH 5.5, 95°C, 10min), purified by RP-HPLC, and verified by TLC (RCP>95%). The TF2 bsAb (anti-CEA/anti-HSG) was produced via DNL and Protein A purification.

name: ²¹³Bi-IMP288
radiochemical_method_description: ²¹³Bi-DOTA-IMP288 was prepared by eluting ²¹³Bi from a ²²⁵Ac/²¹³Bi generator (0.1 M HCl-0.1 M NaI), then reacting with IMP288 (0.14–0.56 nmol dual-HSG peptide) in TRIS-ascorbate buffer (pH 9.0, 95°C, 10min). After DTPA quenching, the product was purified by Oasis HLB column (ethanol elution). The TF2 bsAb (anti-CEA/anti-HSG trivalent) was separately produced via DNL and Protein A purification.

name: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-M5A
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-M5A was prepared under GMP by conjugating humanized M5A antibody with NHS-DOTA (~8 DOTA/antibody) in PBS (2h RT), purified by Superdex 200. Labeling with 20 MBq ⁶⁴CuCl₂ (from ⁶⁴Ni cyclotron) in PBS (pH 7.0, 42°C, 1h) was verified by TLC (RCP>90%, bound Rf=1.0).

name: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-Cys-diabody
radiochemical_method_description: ⁶⁴Cu-DOTA-Cys-diabody was expressed in NS0 cells (C-terminal LGGC) and purified by anion exchange/hydroxyapatite chromatography. Site-specific labeling included TCEP reduction, DOTA-GLGK-HVS conjugation, and ⁶⁴Cu labeling (citrate pH 5.5, 43°C, 1h), with EDTA quenching and HPLC purification.

name: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-BsAb
radiochemical_method_description: ¹¹¹In-DOTA-BsAb was prepared by conjugating purified BsAb (2 mg) with NHS-DOTA (50:1 molar ratio) in PBS (pH 7.4, 4°C, 18h), purified by Superdex 200 (4–6 DOTA/BsAb). Labeling with ¹¹¹InCl₃ (5.4 μCi/μg) in citrate buffer (pH 5.5, 37°C, 1h) was quenched with EDTA and purified by Superdex 200.

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-IMP288
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-IMP288 was prepared by adding 20 nmol IMP288 to 1200 MBq ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ (pH-adjusted to 5.5 with ammonium acetate), heating at 100°C for 20 minutes, quenching with DTPA/ascorbate, and verifying by TLC (bound Rf=0–0.1).

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz-1
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz probes (Tz-1) were labeled by reacting 10 nmol Tz with ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ (10–40 MBq) in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, 30min RT, no heating to prevent degradation). No HPLC purification was needed, and the product was diluted in saline and verified by ITLC-SG (bound Rf=0).

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz-2
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz probes (Tz-2) were labeled by reacting 10 nmol Tz with ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ (10–40 MBq) in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, 30min RT, no heating to prevent degradation). No HPLC purification was needed, and the product was diluted in saline and verified by ITLC-SG (bound Rf=0).

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz-3
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz probes (Tz-3) were labeled by reacting 10 nmol Tz with ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ (10–40 MBq) in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, 30min RT, no heating to prevent degradation). No HPLC purification was needed, and the product was diluted in saline and verified by ITLC-SG (bound Rf=0).

name: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz-4
radiochemical_method_description: ¹⁷⁷Lu-Tz probes (Tz-4) were labeled by reacting 10 nmol Tz with ¹⁷⁷LuCl₃ (10–40 MBq) in acetate buffer (pH 5.5, 30min RT, no heating to prevent degradation). No HPLC purification was needed, and the product was diluted in saline and verified by ITLC-SG (bound Rf=0).

name: 89Zr-malDFO-GK1.5 cDb
radiochemical_method_description: Site-specific conjugation of malDFO to reduced cDb, followed by 89Zr labeling using 89Zr-oxalate. Purification via PD-10 or Bio-Spin 6 columns.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NODAGA-SNA006
radiochemical_method_description: Site-specific conjugation of NODAGA-maleimide to the C-terminal cysteine of SNA006, followed by radiolabeling with 68Ga.

name: [89Zr]Zr-DFO-REGN5054
radiochemical_method_description: REGN5054 was conjugated with p-SCN-Bn-DFO. The conjugate was purified and then radiolabeled with [89Zr]Zr-oxalate in HEPES buffer (pH 7.4) at room temperature for 45 minutes. The product was purified via size exclusion chromatography (PD-10 column)

name: 68Ga-NODAGA-SNA006
radiochemical_method_description: Site-specific conjugation of NODAGA-maleimide to C-terminal cysteine of SNA006-GSC. Radiolabeling with ⁶⁸GaCl₃ in sodium acetate buffer (pH=4) at 37°C for 15 minutes, purified via PD-10 column.

name: [89Zr]Zr-malDFO-169 cDb
radiochemical_method_description: The 169 cDb was conjugated site-specifically to the bifunctional chelator maleimide-DFO (malDFO) via engineered cysteine thiols. The conjugate was then radiolabeled with [89Zr]Zr-oxalate. Specific reaction conditions (buffer, time, temperature) are not detailed in the provided text but are referenced to be in the Supplementary Information.

name: [89Zr]Zr-IAB42M1-14
radiochemical_method_description: 89Zr-oxalic acid was neutralized with Na₂CO₃. ⁸⁹Zr was complexed with Df-IAB42M1-14 in HEPES buffer (pH 7.0) at 37°C for 1 hour. The product was purified using a 30 kDa Vivaspin filter.
radiochemical_purity: Determined by radio-TLC and analytical size exclusion chromatography

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-anti-CD8
radiochemical_method_description: The minibody (IAB22M2C) was conjugated with p-SCN-NOTA. NOTA-anti-CD8 was then radiolabeled with [64Cu]CuCl2 in NH4Ac buffer at pH 5.5 and 42°C for 30 min, followed by purification using a size exclusion spin column.

name: [89Zr]Zr-DFO-PEG-anti-CD8 VHH and [89Zr]Zr-DFO-PEG-anti-CD11b VHH
radiochemical_method_description: PEGylated-VHH-DFO conjugate was incubated with [89Zr]Zr4+ in Hepes buffer (pH 7.5) for 60 min at room temperature. The product was purified using a PD-10 size-exclusion column

name: [64Cu]NOTA-CD8a
radiochemical_method_description: NOTA-CD8a-F(ab)₂ incubated with [64Cu]CuCl2in acetate buffer (pH 5.5) with gentisic acid, purified via PD-10 column

name: [89Zr]Zr-ZED8
radiochemical_method_description: CED8 conjugated with 89Zr(IV) oxalate in ammonium acetate buffer, purified via PD-10 column, formulated in succinate/sucrose/polysorbate buffer

name: [68Ga]Ga-NOTA-SNA006a
radiochemical_method_description: 68GaCl₃ eluted in 0.1 M HCl, mixed with sodium acetate and NOTA-SNA006a, incubated at room temperature for 10 min, purified via PD-10 column

name: [89Zr]Zr-ZED88082A
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling of DFO-conjugated CED88004S with 89Zr-oxalate under GMP conditions.

name: [89 Zr]Zr-DFO-LEM2/15
radiochemical_method_description: Conjugation of DFO-NCS to LEM2/15 at pH 9.0, 37°C, 40 min. Radiolabeling with 89Zr in HEPES buffer, pH 7.0–7.5, 90 min at room temperature. Purification via PD-10 column.

name: [89 Zr]Zr-DFO-LEM2/15
radiochemical_method_description: 1.Conjugation of DFO-NCS to LEM2/15 at pH 9.0, 37°C, 40 min. Radiolabeling with 89Zr in HEPES buffer, pH 7.0–7.5, 90 min at room temperature. Purification via PD-10 column.2.Radiolabeling of DOTA-peptides with ⁶⁸Ga using fractionated elution from a ⁶⁸Ge/⁶⁸Ga generator. Reaction in HEPES buffer at 90°C for 5 min. Purification via C18 cartridge.

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-AF7p
radiochemical_method_description: 1.Conjugation of DFO-NCS to LEM2/15 at pH 9.0, 37°C, 40 min. Radiolabeling with 89Zr in HEPES buffer, pH 7.0–7.5, 90 min at room temperature. Purification via PD-10 column.2.Radiolabeling of DOTA-peptides with ⁶⁸Ga using fractionated elution from a ⁶⁸Ge/⁶⁸Ga generator. Reaction in HEPES buffer at 90°C for 5 min. Purification via C18 cartridge.

name: [177 Lu]Lu-Gemini
radiochemical_method_description: Gemini precursor reacted with 177LuCl₃ under standard radiolabeling conditions. Purification via reverse-phase HPLC.

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-LEM2/15
radiochemical_method_description: Conjugation of chelator to antibody, followed by radiolabeling with 89Zr or 177Lu using PD-10 desalting column for purification.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-Acd40
radiochemical_method_description: aCD40-NOTA was incubated with [64Cu]CuCl2 in 0.1 M CH3CO2Na buffer (pH 5.5) for 1 hour at 40°C.

name: [211At]NpG-GGN4c
radiochemical_method_description: Precursor 3 reacted with [211At]At- in acetonitrile with sodium ascorbate, followed by copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) with azido-peptide N3-GGN4.

name: [177Lu]Lu-ABD
radiochemical_method_description: ABD and Gemini precursors chelated 177Lu efficiently. Reaction with [177Lu]LuCl3. For Gemini, after radiolabeling, remaining empty DOTA chelators are filled with non-radioactive Lu ("backfilling").

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-IMB16
radiochemical_method_description: The monoclonal antibody IMB16 was first conjugated with the bifunctional chelators p-SCN-Bn-NOTA or p-SCN-Bn-DOTA via reaction with lysine residues. The conjugated antibodies were then radiolabeled with 64CuCl2 or 177LuCl3, respectively, in ammonium acetate buffer (pH 5.5) at 45°C for 60 minutes (64Cu) or 37°C for 60 minutes (177Lu).

name: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-PEG4-Tz-TCO-PEG3-anti-CD25 Ab
radiochemical_method_description: The method involved a two-step process: 1) High-temperature (90 °C) radiolabeling of DOTA-PEG4-tetrazine with 177Lu, and 2) Conjugation of the [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-PEG4-Tz to the TCO-functionalized anti-CD25 antibody via a rapid, room-temperature, bioorthogonal inverse electron-demand Diels-Alder (IEDDA) click chemistry reaction.

name: [89Zr]Zr-DFO-AC-10
radiochemical_method_description: The monoclonal antibody AC-10 was conjugated with the chelator desferrioxamine B (DFO). The DFO-AC-10 conjugate was then radiolabeled with 89Zr in 1 M HEPES buffer at pH 6.8-7.0 for 60 minutes at room temperature.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-YY146
radiochemical_method_description: The monoclonal antibody YY146 was conjugated with the bifunctional chelator p-SCN-Bn-NOTA. The conjugated antibody was then radiolabeled with 64CuCl2 in ammonium acetate buffer (pH 5.5) at 37°C for 1 hour.

name: [212Pb]Pb-TCMC-OI-3
radiochemical_method_description: The article mentions the "preclinical evaluation of 212Pb-TCMC-OI-3" and cites reference 53 (Baidoo, K. E., Milenic, D. E. & Brechbiel, M. W. Nucl. Med. Biol. 40, 592–599, 2013) for the methodology of labeling proteins and peptides with lead-212.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-ABDB6
radiochemical_method_description: ABDB6 was conjugated with p-SCN-Bn-NOTA and then labeled with 64Cu. The entire labeling process, including purification using size-exclusion column chromatography (PD-10 column), took 50 ± 10 minutes.

name: [68Ga]Ga-CBP8
radiochemical_method_description: 68GaCl3, eluted from a 68Ge/68Ga generator, was mixed with an aqueous solution of CBP8 (or CNBP) and sodium acetate buffer (pH 5.5). The mixture was heated to 95 °C for 15 min, cooled, and the pH was adjusted to 6.5-7 with sterile PBS and NaOH

name: [68Ga]Ga-NOTA-PFCM01
radiochemical_method_description: PFCM01 was conjugated with NOTA and then radiolabeled with 68Ga. The labeled nanobodies were purified using PD-10 columns.

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-TSHR-Ab
radiochemical_method_description: The human antibody TSHR-Ab (K1-70) was conjugated with the bifunctional chelator NOTA-Bn-NCS under basic aqueous conditions at 37 °C for 60 min. The conjugate NOTA-TSHR-Ab was then radiolabeled with [64Cu]Cu2+ at 37 °C for an additional 60 min.

name: [89Zr]Zr-Df-YY146
radiochemical_method_description: YY146 was conjugated to deferoxamine (Df) via an amine-isothiocyanate reaction. The conjugated antibody (Df-YY146) was then radiolabeled with 89Zr in HEPES buffer (pH=7.0) at 37 °C for 1 h.

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-TNYL-RAW
radiochemical_method_description: The peptide TNYL-RAW was conjugated with the bifunctional chelator DOTA at its N-terminus to form DOTA-TNYL-RAW. This conjugate was then radiolabeled with 64Cu under heating conditions.

name: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-Lys-hAb47
radiochemical_method_description: The anti-EphB4 antibodies (hAb47 and hAb131) were conjugated with the bifunctional chelator DOTA through lysine, cysteine, or oligosaccharide residues on the antibody. The resulting conjugates were then radiolabeled with 64Cu by incubating the reaction mixture at 40°C for 1 hour. The labeled products were purified using a PD-10 column.

name: [99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-P149-QY
radiochemical_method_description: The peptide P149-QY was radiolabeled with 99mTc using two methods: 1) Direct labeling, which resulted in high yields (ca. 98%) but low specific activity. 2) Using HYNIC as a bifunctional chelator and tricine as a co-ligand, which allowed for high specific activity. For the HYNIC method, tricine (0.05 mg/μg peptide) was used, and labeling was performed with a 1-hour incubation at 50°C, achieving yields of ca. 98%. The free sulfhydryl groups (Cys8 and Cys21) were protected (blocked) with EM (likely iodoacetamide or a similar agent) to prevent them from interfering with labeling and to preserve biological activity.

name: [99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-PEG4-EPH-3
radiochemical_method_description: The cold precursor HYNIC-PEG4-EPH-3 was synthesized using solid-phase synthesis and purified by semi-preparative HPLC. Radiolabeling with 99mTc was performed using a co-ligand system of TPPTS and tricine to stabilize the complex.

name: [68Ga]AJ201
radiochemical_method_description: AJ201 was labeled with 68Ga in sodium acetate buffer. The structure of AJ210 is not fully detailed, but it is implied to be a DOTA-conjugated version of the bicyclic peptide for 225Ac labeling.

name: [68Ga]Ga-NOTA-ETN
radiochemical_method_description: The cold precursor ETN was synthesized with a NOTA chelator attached to its N-terminus. Radiolabeling was performed using the [18F][AlF]2+ cation method, where 68Ga is complexed with NOTA in the presence of aluminum fluoride. The final product was identified using high-resolution mass spectrometry (HR-MS).

name: [89Zr]Zr-EphA2 mAb
radiochemical_method_description: The EphA2 mAb was conjugated with DFO or NOTA chelators. [89Zr]Zr-EphA2 mAb was prepared by reacting the DFO-conjugated mAb with purified 89Zr-oxalate in HEPES/Na2CO3 buffer at 27°C. [177Lu]Lu-EphA2 mAb was prepared by reacting the NOTA-conjugated mAb with [177Lu]LuCl3 in sodium acetate buffer at 44°C, followed by a reaction with sodium ascorbate.

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-SD01
radiochemical_method_description: The cyclic peptide SD01 and the linear control peptide YSA were conjugated to the DOTA chelator. The DOTA-peptide conjugates were then labeled with 68Ga in a suitable buffer (implied by standard procedures).

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-PACAP
radiochemical_method_description: The PACAP analogue peptide was conjugated to either DOTA or NODAGA using solid-phase peptide synthesis. The conjugates were then radiolabeled with 68GaCl3 eluted from a 68Ge/68Ga generator in 0.1 M HCl. The NODAGA conjugate showed faster labeling kinetics at room temperature, while the DOTA conjugate required higher temperatures (50-90°C).

name: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-AE105-NH2
radiochemical_method_description: The peptides were radiolabeled with 68Ga from a 68Ge/68Ga generator using a cation-exchange chromatography approach or a fractionated elution approach. The fractionated elution approach was found to be superior, especially for NODAGA-AE105-NH2, as it produced pure products. Purification was performed using a Sep-Pak Light column.

name: [99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-SWL
radiochemical_method_description: HYNIC-SWL was dissolved in saline and incubated with tricine/EDDA solution. 99mTcO4- from a 99Mo/99mTc generator and SnCl2·2H2O were added, and the mixture was incubated at 100°C for 10 minutes. The product was purified using a Sep-Pak C-18 cartridge.

name: [111In]In-CHX-A″-DTPA-DS-8895a
radiochemical_method_description: DS-8895a was conjugated with the chelators CHX-A″-DTPA (for 111In) and Df-Bz-NCS (for 89Zr). Radiolabeling was performed: 111In-CHX-A″-DTPA-DS-8895a at 37°C for 1 hour; 125I-DS-8895a using Iodogen method; 89Zr-Df-Bz-NCS-DS-8895a in HEPES buffer at 37°C for 1 hour. Quality control included SDS-PAGE and radiochemical purity assessment

name: [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-YY146
radiochemical_method_description: The antibody YY146 was conjugated with p-SCN-Bn-NOTA at a 20:1 molar ratio in PBS (pH 8.5-9.0) for 2 hours at 25-30°C. The conjugate (NOTA-YY146) was purified using PD-10 size exclusion columns. Radiolabeling was achieved by reacting 50–100 μg of NOTA-YY146 with 74–148 MBq of 64CuCl2 in 0.1M sodium acetate buffer (pH 4.5) at 37°C for 30 minutes. The final product, [64Cu]Cu-NOTA-YY146, was purified using PD-10 columns and filtered through a 0.2-μm filter.

name: [211At]At-AAMT
radiochemical_method_description: The synthesis method is not detailed in the provided text. It is implied that 211At is introduced via electrophilic destannylation or similar direct radiohalogenation of a precursor molecule.

name: [211At]At-AAMT-O-Me-L
radiochemical_method_description: The text states that 211At is a halogen nuclide that is relatively easy to label, and techniques for labeling various compounds are being investigated. The compound was developed to be easily labeled. The specific method for [211At]At-AAMT-O-Me-L is not described

name: [89Zr]Zr-DFO-Ab2
radiochemical_method_description: The monoclonal antibody Ab2 was conjugated with the chelator DFO (desferrioxamine B). The conjugate was then radiolabeled with 89Zr in 1 M HEPES buffer at pH 6.8-7.0 for 60 minutes at room temperature.

name: 2-[211At]AAMP
radiochemical_method_description: Prepared from a stannyl precursor via a radioastatination reaction.

name: [211At]At-MABG
radiochemical_method_description: Radiosynthesis of 211At-MABG was performed according to a previously published method with slight modification, using 211At produced by a cyclotron. The compound was labeled with 211At via electrophilic substitution.

name: [177Lu]Lu-EC0800
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA folate EC0800 (10⁻³ M) was mixed with HCl (0.05 M; 100-x mL) and sodium acetate (pH 8.0, 20 mL). 177LuCl3 was added, the solution was heated for 10 min at 95°C, and sodium-diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid solution (5 mM, pH 5, 10 mL) was added to complex unreacted 177Lu(III). Quality control by HPLC.

name: [177Lu]Lu-cm09
radiochemical_method_description: DOTA folate cm09 (10⁻³ M) was mixed with HCl (0.05 M; 100-x mL) and sodium acetate (pH 8.0, 20 mL). 177LuCl3 was added, the solution was heated for 10 min at 95°C, and sodium-diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid solution (5 mM, pH 5, 10 mL) was added to complex unreacted 177Lu(III). Quality control by HPLC.

name: [111In]In-DTPA-folate
radiochemical_method_description: DTPA conjugated to folic acid; labeled with 111In(III) chloride at room temperature per kit-formulated protocol; radiochemical purity determined by thin-layer chromatography.

name: [99mTc]Tc-etarfolatide
radiochemical_method_description: Folate-derived conjugate labeled with 99mTc; radiolabeling method consistent with folate-HYNIC-Tc conjugates

name: [47Sc]Sc-cm10
radiochemical_method_description: 47Sc produced via ⁴⁶Ca(n,γ)⁴⁷Ca→β⁻⁴⁷Sc nuclear reaction; separated by extraction and cation exchange chromatography; cm10 (10⁻³ M) mixed with 47Sc in NH₄Ac/HCl (pH 4.5) and incubated at 95°C for 10 min; 5 mM sodium diethylene triamine pentaacetic acid added to complex free 47Sc(III); quality control by HPLC.

name: [149Tb]Tb-cm09
